title: Curiosity
description:
# Automated Web Search & Scraping System  
### Playwright + DuckDuckGo API + LLM-driven extraction

A production-ready blueprint for a system that **searches the web, visits result pages, extracts structured data with an LLM,** and returns clean JSON—fast, reliable, and auditable.

---

## ✨ Highlights

- **Search-first**: Query DuckDuckGo (API or HTML fallback) to discover relevant URLs.  
- **Headless browsing**: Use **Playwright** to render JavaScript-heavy pages, handle cookies, and emulate devices.  
- **Smart extraction**: An **LLM** converts messy HTML into structured JSON based on a **task schema**.  
- **Resilient pipeline**: Timeouts, retries, backoff, and per-domain throttling.  
- **Pluggable**: Swap search providers, add content normalizers, or integrate vector stores.  
- **Auditable**: Persist raw HTML, normalized text, and final JSON with trace IDs.

---
LLMdump:
Project Name: Curiosity
Tech Stack: LLM, Next.js, Express.js, WebSocket
Timeline: 2025-02-04 to 2025-03-15
Description: Curiosity is an automated web search and scraping system that uses Playwright and the DuckDuckGo API. It integrates LLMs for efficient data extraction and structuring.
Domains: Web Scraping, Chatbots
shortDesc: A production-ready pipeline for fast, reliable, and auditable web search, scraping, and LLM-powered data extraction.

title: Daily Deep Search Newsletter
description: Curiosity is an intelligent information extraction system designed to automate web research. It uses Playwright to simulate human browsing behavior and access dynamic content, and the DuckDuckGo API to retrieve relevant search results without being tracked. The system integrates a large language model (LLM) to analyze, summarize, and structure the scraped content, enabling users to retrieve concise answers and context-aware summaries. With a real-time backend powered by WebSocket and Express.js, Curiosity delivers a seamless and interactive user experience for data querying, making it suitable for building chatbots, research tools, or knowledge assistants.

Project Name: Daily Deep Search Newsletter

Domains: Artificial Intelligence, Web Scraping
shortDesc: Automated daily newsletter generation using LLMs and web scraping for curated updates.

title: ML-Based Face Attendance
techStack: Machine Learning, KNN, Digital Image Processing, Python
description: This project implements a machine learning-powered face recognition attendance system tailored for environments like classrooms or secure workplaces. Leveraging image processing techniques and a KNN classifier, the system can detect, identify, and verify individuals in real-time through webcam input. It minimizes the chances of proxy attendance by matching facial embeddings against stored datasets with high precision. All records are logged into a structured CSV file, making it simple to audit, export, or integrate with other systems. The solution prioritizes speed, accuracy, and user privacy, making it a practical tool for modern digital attendance tracking.
LLMdump:
Project Name: ML-Based Face Attendance
Tech Stack: Machine Learning, KNN, Digital Image Processing, Python
Timeline: 2022-10-04 to 2022-10-05
Description: A facial recognition-based attendance system designed for secure and accurate user identification. Utilizes K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN) and digital image processing techniques to detect and verify faces in real-time, storing attendance data in structured CSV format for easy tracking and analysis.
Domains: ML, Image Processing
shortDesc: Real-time face-recognition attendance using KNN + image processing with CSV audit logs.

title: MongoDB-Based Football Data Manager
description: The MongoDB-Based Football Data Manager is a lightweight yet powerful web application designed to organize and maintain football statistics and metadata. Developed using Node.js for backend logic and MongoDB for persistent storage, the app enables CRUD operations on entities like players, matches, teams, and scores. The frontend, crafted with HTML, CSS, and JavaScript, offers a clean, user-friendly interface for both casual users and sports analysts. This project serves as a practical example of full-stack development with a focus on structured data handling and scalable database integration.

title: IoT and ML-Based Weather Prediction
description: This project is a fusion of embedded IoT technology and machine learning aimed at improving weather forecasting accuracy. A network of environmental sensors collects real-time data on temperature, humidity, atmospheric pressure, and more. This data is then processed through a series of ML models, including ensemble techniques, to generate short-term and medium-range weather predictions. The system is designed for continuous data collection and real-time processing, making it suitable for smart agriculture, city planning, and environmental monitoring. The modular architecture allows it to be deployed in diverse geographical regions with minimal adjustments.

title: NatureNest
description: NatureNest is a digital platform designed to support urban gardening communities by helping members stay organized and connected. The app enables users to manage their garden plots, log harvest data, and monitor activity timelines in real-time. Built with a Django backend and a React.js frontend, it ensures a fast, user-friendly experience. SQL is used for structured storage of garden data, and the system is hosted on Google Cloud Platform (GCP) to ensure reliability and scalability. Whether for community gardens or educational green spaces, NatureNest simplifies the management and collaboration around shared gardening projects.

description: This project is a modern and responsive personal portfolio crafted using the Next.js framework. Designed to highlight technical skills, projects, and achievements, it employs Framer Motion for fluid UI animations and polished transitions. Dynamic content is managed through Supabase with PostgreSQL as the underlying database, allowing seamless updates to project listings and blog posts. Notably, it includes AI-powered features using the Vercel AI SDK — such as a conversational assistant that explains portfolio content. Hosted on Vercel, the site prioritizes performance, interactivity, and a clean developer-focused aesthetic, making it both functional and impressive to visitors.

title: Resume.AI
techStack: LLM, Next.js, Vector Databases, WebSocket, ChromaDB
description: Resume.AI is an AI-driven resume assistant built to deliver an engaging, intelligent way of showcasing professional experience. Powered by large language models and integrated with RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation), the chatbot can answer detailed questions about a candidate’s skills, projects, and accomplishments. The system utilizes ChromaDB, a vector database, to store and semantically search resume content, ensuring accurate and context-aware responses. Next.js handles the frontend experience, while WebSocket enables real-time conversations. Resume.AI is a modern tool designed for both job seekers and recruiters who want a smarter, interactive alternative to static resumes.

title: TLDR
description: TLDR is a lightweight Chrome extension that provides instant, AI-powered summaries of web pages. The tool integrates a large language model (LLM) with a Flask backend to analyze page content and generate short, digestible summaries. Built using JavaScript and Python, TLDR activates with a single click in the browser, sending the page content to the backend, where it’s processed and summarized before being returned to the user. This project focuses on simplifying content consumption and saving time for users who need quick insights from lengthy articles, blogs, or reports.

title: T.rex: Electronics Circuits Game
description: T.rex: Electronics Circuits Game is an educational browser game designed to make electronics learning engaging and fun. Inspired by the classic offline T.rex game, this version incorporates interactive quizzes and challenges centered on FET (Field-Effect Transistor) theory. Developed using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript, it runs smoothly in any modern web browser without requiring installations. Players guide a dino character through various levels, where each obstacle corresponds to a question or circuit logic puzzle. This project blends gamification with technical education, making it ideal for students or beginners exploring electronics fundamentals.
